steps.title: Identify what the hypothesis predicts
steps.content: The hypothesis claims an inverse relationship: smaller birds should associate more with other species for predator detection, while larger birds should remain more solitary.

steps.title: Identify which observation confirms the prediction
steps.content:
Choice A shows the larger woodpecker observed alone or in single-species flocks and the smaller gnatwren frequently joining multispecies flocks, confirming the inverse relationship.

Choice C reverses this pattern, contradicting the hypothesis.

Choices B and D test unrelated factors instead of body size.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
